title: Menu-bar agent, slide-down history overlay & multi-window editor
goal: Re-architect Photonz to be a CleanShot-style resident MENU-BAR AGENT, not a window-first app. The agent always runs (user can Quit it), owns capture + global hotkeys + the updater, and surfaces history as a GLOBAL top-of-screen slide-down overlay. The editor becomes on-demand and MULTI-WINDOW (one window per image; editing a history item opens a new window or focuses the existing one). This replaces phase-9's single-window, in-editor history carousel. Design: docs/design/capture.md. Keep capture/history/updater LOGIC in testable core/store types; the NSStatusItem/NSPanel/window shell stays thin. TDD for PhotonzCore/Render/CaptureStore changes.

tasks.title: App split: resident menu-bar AppCoordinator + per-window EditorState
tasks.notes: DONE 2026-06-19. AppState → EditorState (per-window: History/ImageStore/DocumentRenderer/document; capture removed). NEW AppCoordinator (@MainActor @Observable, Sources/Photonz/AppCoordinator.swift) = resident root: owns CaptureCenter (capture+hotkeys+CaptureStore), window-open intents, About/Open-panel (windowless), openWindowAction closure. NEW EditorWindowID enum {capture(UUID)|file(URL)|fresh(UUID)|clipboard(UUID)} = window identity; EditorState.seed(from:capture:) loads the doc once per window (guards on document==nil so window REUSE = focus-existing, no reload). PhotonzApp rewritten: WindowGroup(for: EditorWindowID.self) + .defaultLaunchBehavior(.suppressed) (KEY: value-typed WindowGroup STILL auto-opens one window at launch — suppressed fixes it → 0 windows at launch, verified) + .commands{EditorCommands}; menu commands target the focused window via @FocusedValue(\.editorState) (EditorCommands.swift, FocusedValues ext). AGENT lifecycle: AppDelegate (NSApplicationDelegateAdaptor) applicationDidFinishLaunching→coordinator.start() [setActivationPolicy(.accessory)+capture.start()]; applicationShouldTerminateAfterLastWindowClosed=false; build-app.sh Info.plist adds LSUIElement. DECISION/DEVIATION: used SwiftUI MenuBarExtra (not raw NSStatusItem) for the menu-bar item — its always-rendered LABEL captures @Environment(\.openWindow) at launch (the .menu content is lazy, can't), giving the windowless agent a way to spawn editor windows. Same UX; 11.2 fleshes out the menu. VERIFIED headlessly: swift build clean, 381 tests green, app bundle launches as ApplicationType=UIElement with 0 windows, stays alive windowless, opens an editor window on demand (file-open through onOpenURL). NOT verifiable here (needs user — a11y/screen-recording denied): menu-bar dropdown clicks, global hotkeys firing with no key window, openWindowAction-driven New/Open/edit-capture window opening + window REUSE/focus-existing, focused-editor menu routing. The in-editor HistoryPanel (phase-9 carousel) still exists, now reading coordinator.capture; 11.4 replaces it with the global overlay.

tasks.title: Status-item menu (NSStatusItem)
tasks.notes: DONE 2026-06-20. Built on the MenuBarExtra (not raw NSStatusItem) decided in 11.1 — its label captures openWindow for the windowless agent. MenuBarMenu (Sources/Photonz/PhotonzApp.swift) full set, grouped by dividers: Capture Region (⌘⇧4) / Capture Full Screen (⌘⇧3) / Record Screen·Video… (DISABLED, phase 12, with .help) — Show/Hide History (⌘⇧H toggle, label reflects coordinator.isHistoryShown) — New Window / New from Clipboard / Open… — Check for Updates… (11.6, label flips to 'Checking for Updates…' + disabled while in flight) / Preferences… (DISABLED stub, later phase) / About Photonz — Quit Photonz (⌘Q). Icon = SF Symbol camera.viewfinder (template by default). Every action routes through AppCoordinator so it works with no editor window. Also added 'Check for Updates…' to the editor windows' app menu (EditorCommands .appInfo group, next to About). VERIFIED: swift build clean, 398 tests green, app bundle launches as ApplicationType=UIElement (lsappinfo) windowless. NEEDS USER: actual dropdown clicks + the disabled items being greyed (menu-rendering not drivable headlessly — Accessibility denied here).

tasks.title: System shortcut overrides ⌘⇧3 (full) / ⌘⇧4 (region) from the agent
tasks.notes: DONE 2026-06-21 (code complete; TCC grant still NEEDS USER). Carbon global hotkeys (HotkeyCenter) register ⌘⇧3=full, ⌘⇧4=region, ⌘⇧5=record (phase 12), ⌘⇧H=history — all fired from the resident agent with no window (CaptureCenter.start). System-Screenshots conflict is documented in HotkeyCenter's header (our hotkeys start firing once the user disables System Settings → Keyboard → Shortcuts → Screenshots) and the menu items work regardless of hotkey state (Capture menu + menu-bar dropdown route through AppCoordinator). Permission flow: ensurePermission() (CGPreflightScreenCaptureAccess gate) surfaces the overlay hint + primePermissionRegistration (CGRequest + an SCK query, which is what lists Photonz in System Settings) + opens the Screen Recording pane; an explicit 'Request Screen Recording Access…' menu item too. NEEDS USER (can't grant/observe TCC headlessly on this box): confirm ⌘⇧3/⌘⇧4/⌘⇧5 fire with no key window after disabling the system Screenshot bindings, and that Photonz appears in Screen & System Audio Recording and capture/record succeed once granted.

tasks.title: Global slide-down history overlay (⌘⇧H) — replaces the in-editor carousel
tasks.notes: DONE 2026-06-19 (built early — the menu-bar agent was windowless after 11.1 so the old in-editor carousel had nowhere to render; ⌘⇧H/Show History appeared dead). Pure geometry = HistoryOverlayLayout (PhotonzCore, 7 TDD tests): given a screen visibleFrame + height, yields presentedFrame (centered, pinned topInset below the top edge) + hiddenFrame (slid fully above) — only y differs, so show/hide is a pure slide; honors secondary-display origins. Shell = HistoryOverlayController (AppKit): borderless non-activating floating HistoryOverlayPanel (canBecomeKey=true so Esc routes), slide-DOWN+fade-IN on show / slide-UP+fade-OUT on hide via NSAnimationContext (easeOut 0.34 / easeIn 0.24); dismiss on Esc (local keyDown monitor, keyCode 53), click-away (local mouse monitor: event.window !== panel → hide; global monitor for other-app clicks), or re-toggle; onDismiss keeps coordinator.isHistoryShown in sync. Content = HistoryOverlay (SwiftUI, Liquid glass): newest-first strip from CaptureStore, per item Copy (→hide), Edit (→coordinator.editCapture opens/focuses editor window, hide), Delete (store.remove), and drag-the-PNG-out (.onDrag NSItemProvider(contentsOf: store.fileURL)); shows the screen-recording permission hint. REMOVED the phase-9 HistoryPanel.swift + the EditorView isHistoryVisible block; CaptureCenter lost isHistoryVisible/toggleHistory/revealHistory/flashHistoryIfActive and gained onToggleHistory/onRequestHistory closures the coordinator wires (⌘⇧H + permission). Capture no longer auto-pops history (Quick Access Overlay 11.7 will own post-capture feedback). CaptureStore.fileURL made internal for drag-out. VERIFIED at runtime (env-guarded debug trigger, since the Carbon ⌘⇧H needs Accessibility we lack headless): panel appears 1100×196 at layer 3 (floating), X=314 on a 1728px screen (centered) and Y=41 top-left = 8px below the 33px menu bar (topInset honored); debug hook then removed. 388 tests green; app bundle launches as UIElement agent with 0 windows. NEEDS USER: confirm the slide animation feel, Esc/click-away dismiss, Edit-opens-a-window, and drag-out — interactive bits not drivable headlessly.

tasks.title: Multi-window editor + edit round-trip (open-new vs focus-existing)
tasks.notes: DONE 2026-06-21. Open-new-vs-focus-existing was already free from 11.1 (value-typed WindowGroup(for: EditorWindowID.self); openWindow(value:) reuses the window for an id, opens fresh otherwise; multiple editors independent; .file opens arbitrary image/.photonz in their own windows). This task added the SAVE-BACK round-trip: EditorState now tracks sourceCaptureID (set in seed() for the .capture case) + compositeImage() (1× flattened render). New menu command 'Save to Capture History' (⌥⌘S, EditorCommands File group, disabled with no doc) → AppCoordinator.saveEditedCapture(sourceID:image:): if the source capture is still present it presents an NSAlert with Override Original / Save as New / Cancel (override = CaptureStore.replace(id:with:) rewrites the PNG bytes in place, identity + history position unchanged; save-as-new = store.add → new top entry); a doc with no live source just adds a new entry. The overlays observe CaptureStore so they refresh. CaptureStore.replace is app-side (no new core type needed — override is a pure disk rewrite; existing CaptureHistory tests cover ordering). VERIFIED: builds clean, 425 tests green. NEEDS USER (interactive): the alert flow + that Override visibly updates the history/quick-access thumbnail and Save-as-new adds a second entry.

tasks.title: Check for Updates
tasks.notes: DONE 2026-06-20. Lightweight custom check, no Sparkle. CORE (TDD, 10 tests): SemanticVersion (PhotonzCore/SemanticVersion.swift) — Comparable major.minor.patch, tolerant parse ('v0.2.0', whitespace, missing minor/patch→0, rejects garbage/4-component), numeric (not lexical) ordering; UpdateAvailability(current:latest:) enum = .upToDate / .updateAvailable(v), offers an update only when latest > current (a dev build ahead of the release stays .upToDate). SHELL: UpdateChecker (Sources/Photonz/UpdateChecker.swift) reads running version from Bundle CFBundleShortVersionString (stamped from VERSION by build-app.sh; falls back to 0.0.0 for plain swift-build dev runs), fetches https://dzearing.github.io/photonz/version.json (reloadIgnoringLocalCacheData, 15s timeout), decodes {version}, returns .upToDate/.updateAvailable/.failed(msg). AppCoordinator.checkForUpdates() (guarded by isCheckingForUpdates) presents an NSAlert: up-to-date → OK; available → Download…(opens github.com/dzearing/photonz/releases/latest)/Later; failed → warning. Wired into both the menu-bar dropdown (11.2) and EditorCommands app menu. VERIFIED: live version.json reachable, returns 0.2.0 == current VERSION so a release build reports up-to-date; bundle stamps CFBundleShortVersionString=0.2.0; 398 tests green. NEEDS USER: the 'update available' alert path (needs a newer published version, or run a lower-versioned dev bundle) + Download button opening the release page.

tasks.title: Quick Access Overlay (post-capture floating thumbnail)
tasks.notes: SUPERSEDED + REMOVED 2026-06-21 per user feedback: the corner toast was redundant with the slide-down history overlay (the toast auto-dismissed and wasn't easily recallable, while history is ⌘⇧H-recallable). Replaced by: on capture/recording complete, SHOW the history overlay with the NEWEST entry HIGHLIGHTED (accent ring + glow). Deleted QuickAccessController/QuickAccessOverlay (app) + QuickAccessLayout/ScreenCorner (PhotonzCore) + its 7 tests; AppCoordinator.flashNewCapture(id) sets highlightedCaptureID then showHistory(); hideHistory clears the highlight. Save-to-File for images moved onto the history image cells (was the toast's only unique action). Tile-tap-to-play + video play/save-export live on the history cells now. ORIGINAL (history): DONE 2026-06-20. CleanShot signature feature, built on the proven 11.4 pattern (testable geometry core + thin AppKit shell). CORE (TDD, 7 tests): QuickAccessLayout (PhotonzCore/QuickAccessLayout.swift) + ScreenCorner enum (Codable/CaseIterable, ready to become a user pref) — given a display visibleFrame + size + corner + margin, yields restingFrame (docked in the corner) and hiddenFrame (slid off the nearest horizontal edge: below for bottom corners, above for top) for the slide-in/out; only y differs; honors secondary-display origins. SHELL: QuickAccessController (AppKit) — borderless NON-activating floating QuickAccessPanel (canBecomeKey=false, a passive HUD that never steals focus), slide+fade in (easeOut 0.32) / out (easeIn 0.22); AUTO-CLOSE via DispatchWorkItem (6s default; setHovering(true) cancels it while the pointer is over the card, setHovering(false) restarts a 1.5s grace); a second capture while one is up swaps the hosting rootView + resets the timer (no re-animate). CONTENT: QuickAccessOverlay (SwiftUI, Liquid glass, IconActionButtonStyle) — thumbnail + Copy (→hide), Save…(NSSavePanel writes the PNG bytes so overwrite works, →hide), Edit (→coordinator.editCapture opens/focuses editor window, →hide), Delete (store.remove, →hide), drag-the-PNG-out (.onDrag), .onHover→controller. TRIGGER: CaptureCenter gained onCaptureComplete((CaptureEntry)) fired after every store.add (full-screen + rect); AppCoordinator wires it to showQuickAccess(id) + adds hideQuickAccess()/saveCaptureToDisk(). VERIFIED: 405 tests green; env-guarded headless self-test (synthetic capture → showQuickAccess) confirmed the panel's ACTUAL on-screen frame (24,80,232,196) == computed bottom-left restingFrame on the main display (visibleFrame y56+margin24=80), then removed. NEEDS USER (interactive, not headless): real post-capture pop, slide/auto-close feel, hover-pauses-timeout, the 4 actions + drag-out. Edit currently opens the editor but the save-BACK round-trip (override vs save-as-new) is 11.5, still pending. Corner is fixed bottom-left for now (ScreenCorner pref = later). Pin-to-screen action (11.8) not yet added to this overlay.

tasks.title: Pin-to-screen: float a capture above all windows
tasks.notes: DONE 2026-06-20. CORE (TDD, 6 tests): PinnedImageMetrics (PhotonzCore) — fittedSize(imageSize:maxDimension:) aspect-fits into a square, never upscaling, rounds to whole pts; clampOpacity into [0.2,1.0]. SHELL: PinnedWindowController (AppKit) owns N independent pinned windows — each a borderless+resizable NSWindow, level .floating (above normal windows), isMovableByWindowBackground (drag from anywhere), clear/opaque=false, hasShadow, aspectRatio locked to content, minSize 80², canJoinAllSpaces+fullScreenAuxiliary; new pins cascade down-left from the top-right inset (28pt × 6 cycle); unpin via the window's own close button (isReleasedWhenClosed=false). CONTENT: PinnedImageView (SwiftUI) — image fills the window; on HOVER a close (×) button (top-trailing) + an opacity slider in a glass capsule (bottom) fade in; opacity applies to the IMAGE only (not window alpha) so the controls stay legible while dialing down, clamped via PinnedImageMetrics. WIRED: AppCoordinator.pinCapture(entryID) → pin on activeScreen; Pin buttons (pin SF Symbol) added to BOTH the Quick Access Overlay (11.7, →hide) and the history overlay (11.4, →hideHistory). VERIFIED: 411 tests green; env-guarded headless self-test pinned a 400×250 synthetic image twice → 2 floating windows both sized (360,225) == computed fittedSize (0.9 scale), first at top-right inset (1344,835), second cascaded (1372,807) (+28/−28); debug hook removed. NEEDS USER (interactive): drag-to-move, edge-resize keeping aspect, hover controls, opacity slider feel, close-to-unpin.
